title: The 16-Year-Old's Request
desc: A competent 16-year-old requests antidepressant therapy and asks you not to inform her parents, who she says will remove her from school if they know.

patient.name: Imane
patient.condition: Moderate depression — SI denied, no acute risk today

law.text: Rules balance evolving adolescent capacity with parental involvement; emergencies and grave harm alter default duties toward disclosure and protection.

complications.title: Mother arrives unannounced
complications.premise: Reception misdirects the parent to your office.
complications.liveLine: Her mother bursts in: "Why is my daughter seeing psychiatry without me?" Imane freezes.

complications.title: Counselor emails you
complications.premise: School reports falling grades and vague self-harm chat.
complications.liveLine: Email: "We're worried she may be minimizing risk — please confirm she's safe."

complications.title: Pharmacy needs guardian
complications.premise: Local pharmacy will not dispense without parental ID for minors.
complications.liveLine: 📞 Pharmacist: "Unless you change the script or a parent signs, she can't start this week."

complications.title: Father threatens doctor
complications.premise: He says prescribing without consent is malpractice.
complications.liveLine: "If you hide mental treatment from us, my lawyer will file tomorrow — she's our child."

steps.msg: Doctor, I thought about suicide last year but not now. If my parents know I'm on medication, they'll pull me from exams. Can we start treatment and keep this between us?
steps.q: What is your first ethical priority?
steps.hint: Safety assessment and capacity come before secrecy promises:

choices.label: Structured safety & capacity assessment
choices.reaction: Okay… I'll answer honestly if you don't judge me.

choices.label: Promise full secrecy immediately
choices.reaction: I relaxed — maybe too much — you don't even know if I'm safe.

choices.label: Refuse care until parents come
choices.reaction: Then I'll never come back — I'll lie to everyone.

choices.label: Prescribe immediately to keep her engaged
choices.reaction: I'm glad for pills — but we skipped hard questions.

steps.msg: Safety screen is reassuring today. She shows good understanding of meds and risks — and repeats that disclosure could worsen her mental health through family violence ("not hitting, but control").
steps.q: How do you balance confidentiality and parental involvement?
steps.hint: Graduated approaches protect adolescents at risk of harm from disclosure:

choices.label: Offer staged disclosure coaching / trusted mediator
choices.reaction: Maybe… if someone helps my parents hear it without exploding.

choices.label: Forever hide from parents
choices.reaction: I'll rely only on you — that's a lot of pressure on you.

choices.label: Call parents today without her consent
choices.reaction: I told you they'd punish me — I was right.

steps.msg: She agrees to therapy plus medication if follow-up stays predictable. Parents still don't know. Teachers notice absences for visits.
steps.q: How do you close the plan ethically?
steps.hint: Continuity, clear boundaries on confidentiality limits, and documentation:

choices.label: Written care plan + when you must disclose
choices.reaction: I hate limits — but at least I understand them.

choices.label: Off-book appointments cash only
choices.reaction: Even I know that's not real medicine.

choices.label: Coordinate with school nurse only
choices.reaction: If school helps me stay in class, maybe it's okay.

debrief.summary: Adolescent mental health demands careful capacity assessment, honest limits on confidentiality, and creative involvement pathways when family contact carries realistic harm — not blanket lies to parents or reckless promises.
points: Capacity can be partial and topic-specific; reassess when risk changes.
Confidentiality helps care engagement but must yield to imminent safety.
Staged disclosure with mediation often beats binary secrecy-vs-telling.
Document decisions to protect both patient and clinician amid legal ambiguity.
